meta.role: People Analytics & Operations, University Hire (Rotational Program)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) People & Organizational Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people experience and operations—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Analyzed 2,000+ {data|employee survey responses} to turn a messy people question into a clean narrative guiding C-suite decisions
Led a manager-effectiveness {data|analysis}, transforming raw feedback data into executive-ready reports that informed engagement strategy
Owned the analytical arc end-to-end—from pulling the {data|data} to presenting recommendations to senior leadership
Improved {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects spanning survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Upskill an HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ leave cases, refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Redesigned {coord|onboarding} workflows and documentation for 70+ {cross|cross-functional} staff, surfacing process gaps that cut onboarding time 75%
Manage 20+ programs and 10+ {coord|vendors}, keeping {data|program data} clean and refining delivery via {data|feedback analysis}
Prototype {tools|AI-assisted workflows} (ChatGPT, Gemini) to replace manual, repetitive operations tasks and move faster
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global {cross|stakeholders} to identify gaps and design 3 new programs, reaching a 78% enrollment rate
Optimized {coord|onboarding} processes for new hires across 3 global locations via process analysis and clearer job responsibilities
Facilitated organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation data
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
